messages.content: Tomas have you ever seen a resplendent quetzal??
i just watched this video from monteverde and i cant stop thinking about it
Hi Ashley, I have not seen one in person, but they are incredible creatures. Monteverde is one of the best places in the world to find them.
wait have you been to monteverde?
I have not, but my cousin lives near San Jose and has shared many photos from the cloud forests. The quetzal is sacred in many Central American cultures. Truly one of God's most beautiful creations.
thats so cool. do you know what time of year is best to see them
I believe the breeding season is February through June. The males grow those extraordinary tail feathers to attract mates. My cousin says the early morning guided tours have the best chances.
ok that is exactly what i needed to know lol
thank you for indulging my random bird questions
It is never random to be drawn to beauty in nature, Ashley. Let me know if you want my cousin's contact information. He knows the area well.
i might take you up on that!
Good morning, Ashley. I have been thinking about you. How are you holding up this week?
hey tomas
honestly kinda rough
having one of those weeks where everything feels heavy
I am sorry to hear that. Would you like to talk about it, or would you just like to know someone is thinking of you?
thats really kind
its work stuff. someone made me feel really small in a meeting and i cant shake it
That sounds painful. Being dismissed is one of the hardest things, especially when you have worked hard to earn your place.
yeah exactly
seeing dr rivera tmrw so at least theres that
Good. I am glad you have that support. Please remember that one person's behavior does not define your worth.
thank you tomas seriously
sometimes i just need someone to say that
That is what community is for. Do not hesitate to reach out if you need anything this week.
i wont. thanks for checking in
ok random question
if you were going on like a multi day bird hike in a cloud forest what would you bring
Good afternoon, Ashley. That is a fun question. Layers are essential in the cloud forest. It is cool and misty, not at all like Miami. Waterproof jacket, good hiking boots, and a lightweight daypack.
oh wow i didnt realize it was cool there
i was picturing tropical heat lol
Monteverde sits at around 1,400 meters elevation. Temperatures are usually 15 to 20 degrees Celsius. Very different from the lowlands. You will want long pants for the trails as well.
ok this is super helpful. i just got binoculars and im trying to figure out what else i need
Binoculars are a great investment. For the cloud forest, I would also recommend a quick-dry towel and moisture-wicking clothing. Everything gets damp in the mist.
noted. thanks tomas youre the best
Anytime, Ashley. It sounds like you are planning something wonderful. Remember to enjoy the preparation as much as the journey itself.
Ok so I need to vent about something
whats up
My car payment went up because apparently my rate was promotional and now it's like $80 more a month?? I literally cannot deal with this
ugh that sucks. did you call the lender
I'm going to but ugh I just hate money stuff. Speaking of which you're like ALWAYS saving. What are you even saving for at this point? You never treat yourself!!
lol i treat myself plenty
just saving, nothing specific
Ashley come ON. You literally put away like half your paycheck. Are you buying a house? A car? Omg are you going on a trip somewhere??
morgan lol no. its just good to have a cushion you know
You sound like a financial planner right now 🙄
i literally am a financial planner
Ha ok fair 😂 But seriously one day you're gonna have to actually LIVE a little. You can't just save forever
i promise im living. how are things with work otherwise
Ugh don't even get me started. My manager changed the campaign timeline AGAIN and now I have to redo all my content calendars by Friday
that sounds annoying
It IS. Anyway I gotta go deal with dinner. Love you sis ❤️
love u too
What if I came to visit you in Miami??
I've been looking at flights and I literaly need to escape Atlanta
wait omg YES
u should come!!
I was thinking maybe that second weekend in March? Like the 14th?
We could do the beach and go out and I can finally meet Miso 🥺
miso would love u lol
let me look at my calendar real quick tho
ok will check tmrw
ok so i checked my schedule
that weekend in mid march is kinda packed for me already
would the first weekend work? like march 7-9
The 7th is a Saturday right
yeah sat thru mon
That could work! I'd just take Monday off
perfect. we can do teh beach and wynwood and all the food spots
YES I need real Cuban food not this sad Atlanta version 😂
Can I crash at your place?? Or should I get a hotel
couch obviously lol. chloe is out late most nights
You're the best 💕 Let me look at flights and confirm!
sounds good!
March 7-9!! Saturday morning flight in, Monday afternoon back
Spirit but it was $118 round trip 🙃
YESSS

I literally cannot wait. I need this so bad
same its been forever since ive seen u

haha hes gonna love u

wynwood! so much to do around here
The murals!! I've seen so many pics. This is going to be SO good ❤️
its gonna be great 💕

and comfy shoes for walking

ahhh!!! getting ready
text me when ur at baggage
Grabbing my bag now. Already so warm omg

yeah thats easiest. 2847 nw 2nd ave apt 4b

Good evening, everyone. I am pleased to share the details for our retreat at Oleta River State Park on February 22-23.
Day 1, Saturday: Meet at the park entrance at 8:00 AM. Morning hike on the mangrove trail, devotional at the picnic pavilion, afternoon kayaking. Evening campfire with worship and reflection.
Day 2, Sunday: Sunrise prayer walk at 6:30 AM. Breakfast at 7:30. Nature meditation and journaling. We will wrap up around noon.
Packing list: comfortable hiking shoes, water bottle, sunscreen, bug spray, swimwear or quick-dry clothes for kayaking, journal, Bible, and a snack to share.
